content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 17,
1994
Mike Rothschild
Mojave Desert Air Quality Management District
15428 Civic Drive
Suite 200
Victorville,
CA
92392—2383
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—94—231
Dear Mr. Rothschild:
This is in response to your request for advice concerning
your responsibilities under the conflict—of—interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
OUESTIONS
As Chairman and member of the Governing Board of the Mojave
Desert Air Quality Management District (“district”),
may you
participate in the following situations which are likely to occur
in the near future:
1.
The deliberation and vote on the district’s annual
budget.
The budget will include funding for all district
positions including the proposed position of the Chief District
Counsel.
2.
Specific deliberation and vote relating to establishing
the position of the Chief District Counsel.
3.
Specific deliberation and-vote relating to Mr.
Quadri’s
consideration for the positionof Chief District Counsel or any
other position.
CONCLUSION
1,2,
& 3.
You are not required to disqualify yourself from
participating in the above decisions.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—94—231
Page
2
FACTS
You were an unsuccessful candidate in the race for the 1994
Republican nomination for the 34th district of the State Assembly.
You are also the Governing Board Chairman of the Mojave Desert Air
Quality Management District.
On April 25,
1994,
Mr.
Fazle Rab Quadri, Acting District
Counseland an employee of the district, contributed $200 to your
fund—raising events.
During May 1994, your campaign organization,
Committee to Elect Mike Rothschild for State Assembly,
was
reorganized and at your invitation, Mr. Quadri became a volunteer
treasurer of your committee.
On June 2,
1994,
Mr.
Quadri loaned
$500 to your committee.
The committee has repaid $100 of the loan
to Mr.
Quadri.
ANALYSIS
Section 82015 states in relevant part:
“Contribution” means a payment,
a forgiveness
of a loan,
a payment of a loan by a third party,
or
an enforceable promise to make a payment except to
the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes.
Section 82015,
(emphasis added).
Section 82044 defines a “payment” as:
[A]
payment, distribution, transfer,
loan,
advance, deposit, gift or other rendering of money,
property, services or anything else of value,
whether tangible or intangible.
Sectidii 82044,
(emphasis added).
Accordingly, the term “contribution” includes a loan made for
political purposes.
You have indicated that Mr.
Quadri loaned $500 to your
committee after he became a volunteer treasurer of your committee.
Since the loan was made for political purposes, the loan is deemed
to be a contribution.
(Section 82015.)
Therefore, the total
amount of contributions Mr.
Quadri made to your committee in the
previous 12 months is in excess of $250.
Section 84308(c) states in relevant part:
Prior to rendering any decision in a
proceeding involving a license,
permit or other
entitlement for use pending before an agency,
each


File No.
A—94—231
Page
3
officer of the agency who received a contribution
within the preceding 12 months in an amount of more
than two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
from a party
or from any participant shall disclose that fact on
the record of the proceeding.
No officer of an
agency shall make, participate in making,
or in any
way attempt to use his or her official position to
influence the decision in a proceeding involving a
license, permit, or other entitlement for use
pending before the agency if the officer has
willfully or knowingly received a contribution in
an amount of more than two hundred fifty dollars
($250) within the preceding 12 months from a party
or his or her agent,
or from any participant, or
his or her agent if the officer knows or has reason
to know that the participant has a financial
interest in the decision,
as that term is described
with respect to public officials in Article 1
(commencing with Section 87100)
of
Chapter 7.
Section 84308,
(emphasis added).
Section 84308 (a) (5)
defines “license, permit or other
entitlement for use” as:
[A] 11 business, professional, trade and land
use licenses and permits and all other entitlements
for use,
including all entitlements for land use,
all contracts (other than competitively bid,
labor,
or personal employment contracts),
and all
franchises.
Section 84308(a)(5),
(emphasis added).
In the Hickling Advice Letter,
No.
A—87—215, the Commission
interpreted the phrase “labor and personal eiñployment contracts”;
it was explained that the exception for “labor and personal
employment contracts” in Section 84308 (a) (5)
was intended to cover
contracts between the governmental agency and its various
employees; that the “labor” contract exception was intended to
cover the rank and file employees and the “personal employment”
contract exception was intended to cover the agency’s management
employees.
Section 84308(c) requires disqualification from participating
in a decision in a proceeding involving a “license, permit or
other entitlement for use” if the public official has received a
contribution in an amount of more than $250 in the preceding 12
months.
You have questioned whether you should disqualify
yourself from participating in decisions affecting the
establishment of the position of Chief District Counsel and Mr.
Quadri’s consideration for such position or any other position.


File No.
A—94—231
Page
4
However,
since the phrase “license, permit,
or other entitlement
for use” excludes “personal employment contracts” you are not
required to disqualify yourself from participating in decisions
affecting the establishment of the position of Chief District
Counsel and Mr. Quadri’s consideration for that or any other
position merely because of Mr. Quadri’s contributions to your
committee.
2
I trust the above discussion responds to your request for
advice.
If you have any other questions related to this matter,
please contact me at 916/322—5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Jeevan Ahuja
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:JA:ak
2
Please note that in addition to Section 84308, Section
87100, et seq., requires a public official to disqualify himself
or herself from participating in a decision if the public official
or,
among others,
a source of income or gifts may be financially
affected by the decision.
I have enclosed a copy of a pamphlet
describing the application of those provisions to public
officials.
